item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having to read the comments to make heads or tails of an article is a no-no for me. I'm sorry but I can't upvote a skip written in a manner so unenjoyably confusing.

item.title: Typos
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2940 attacks all organisms within eyesight" - should be -B

"It was a lot bigger than the normal earthquakes, and it was going though the entire bunker." - through, not though

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: With no indication any of these images or their components have been released under Creative Commons, I've removed the images.

item.title: So um, how do you get in?
item.content:encoded:
In the article it says "The entrance to SCP-2940 is accessible through a stairwell next to each floor's medical bay." and that just doesn't make sense, (unless that's intentional?) because otherwise it's saying the underground bunker is only accessable from inside the underground bunker.
Also, for the sake of clairity I recommend numbering the -a groups instead of having them be denoted by letters as that can lead to confusion, or at least for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "We didn't ask for this" priceless

item.title: Staff Post- Wikiwalk proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: edit made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Title for showing text
- Title for hiding text

To link to another article featuring the same GOI, I propose the following edit:

Light Courier Enterprises: "Hideaway Home!"

to

Light Courier Enterprises: "Hideaway Home!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Inhaled me in like a respirator, though I must admit I do not understand this article completely; especially the role of -B, I think this article could have done without the presence of -B, but I do feel there must have been a sound reason for the author to include it.
Also something I want to question is that, since the title of SCP is "The Light Courier" itself, the author might have intended this as a key article of whole Light Courier story arc, but I don't really feel that much impact. I wonder if it is due to my lack of understanding and I might be able to figure out if I dig in a little more. Well, admittedly, this was the creepiest article among SCPs regarding The Light Courier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ananias is one of the the "Honorable" cast, which became predators after Nachsash, and was crucified and expelled from Eden. He then became unable to manifest physically (in either human incarnation or as some animate construct, which seem to be the basic options) for some reason and was trapped in this thing, where the people also trapped there are forced to sustain him by belief (or something similar). My read, anyway.

He might be the "A" in SCP-1619, especially since he is "The Light Bringer", not sure though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not getting the connection to SCP-2746. Is -B one of the escapees from SCP-2746-1?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm getting strong Fallout vibes from this one, mostly the idea of underground refuge from the apocalypse gone horribly wrong. The text of the ad in particular sounds a lot like some of the Vault-Tec info you can find in-game. That said, it's probably just a coincidence - after all, underground bunkers are a common element in apocalyptic fiction.

I'm not entirely sure how well this works as part of the Pitch Haven setting. I don't think it adds anything, other than some shoehorned references to Salix and Willow. The creepy, semi-religious birdman could have still worked without that connection. Honestly, I feel like 2940-B is unnecessary to the narrative overall. The idea that you can't hide from the apocalypse comes through very clearly already, and I think them dying from the corrupted water fits the story better than being killed by 2940-B.

Also, I agree with what someone else said that putting it by Aokigahara feels unnecessary, unless you're going to use the setting somehow. In fact, I think locating it somewhere English-speaking would make more sense, all things considered. The notes definitely sound more American to me, if that makes sense.

On a more technical note, I find your designations to be very confusing. You have 2940-A, and then you also have the family groups with letter designations. I think it would make more sense to label each family 2940-A1, 2940-A2, and so on. Individual members could be 2940-A1-1 or something.

Overall, it's a neutral vote from me. I like the core idea here very well, but I feel like you've added in too many extraneous details that bog the work down. I apologize if this comes off as overly negative, but I really like the narrative you have here, and I think it could be incredible with a little more work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this has a lot going for it thematically, but I think it needs to be clarified and tightened, possibly shortened. I like the technician's logs particularly.

Downvote, but I could enthusiastic support a rewrite or major edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I didn't want to sleep tonight anyway.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As many others, I couldn't fully understand what was going on until I read some of the explanations here…even when this SCP has gone through a lot of edits and efforts to clarify the events to any reader.

Take this post with a grain of salt, but some codding and format could benefit the organization of the SCP and hopefully make it clearer.

Perhaps adding a Tabview for each SCP instance, and either collapsables or additional tabs for the notes from MTF and for the Addendums. Also, why not put the notes as an addendum?

The SCP-2940-A picture seems to be out of place, shouldn't it be placed before the SCP-2940-B description and the Addendums?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, I like this one a lot. Also, I don't get the complaints. Phrasing is a bit weird here and there (though I didn't take note of where, exactly), and it made my reading of it a bit clunkier than I guess would be ideal, but everything still comes through fine. I don't know what any of the extended mythos for this one is, but I could tell it belonged to some extended mythos I was not entirely aware of, so even that wasn't really a problem.

So, what I could recommend would be to revise some of the phrasing, just in general, and also round out some of the leftovers from previous versions (like, it's not entirely clear why the revisions to the containment procedures happened, or why you'd need to subdue -As)

Anyhoo, it was coo'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, no Internet connection for nearly three weeks!

I thank you for your explanations, Accelerando, but I'm still not bought… Your basic idea for this skip is extremely ambitious, and I'm actually wondering if it's possible to do it justice in a clear and enjoyable way…!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't cuz they're time consuming D:
And I got shit to do

There are methods to do so out there.

Ah, hm. Maybe just rephrase it then, mention imaging or something. The way it's written now, it sounds like people are just looking at it from an underground level.

Using part of this. I don't want to have to say Falco rusticolus more than twice, and I already mention earlier that it's part of the that bird's skeletal system.

You could maybe replace the second with "gyrfalcon" so we get that context without a footnote? It's not elegant, but it could work.

That's a neat idea, but it conflicts with the original image and story I had for the article.

Well, it's the kind of thing that the reader could guess about the document, rather than have it stated outright.

I never clarify if the thing is a man or not, it just uses a male body. Thought I would just leave in there to clarify.

I'm not sure it's that important, honestly. I mean, having a male body, the reader is likely to assume it's male. Again, unless this is a really important detail to the overarching thingy you're trying to do.

The stairwell connecting all floors is like that of any other stairwell; stairs leading to each floor, with a doorway (like a hospital or an office building).

I think I derped on this one, nevermind.

This is because I decided to not use any of Fantem's characters, since I didn't want to mess with the plot he was developing. I am writing a tale to connect this article and 1530, as Ananias, Salix, and Willow were all Edenites. In fact, I didn't know Fantem was writing a canon using all his articles until he told me.

Ah, hm. Well, the connection's in there with the names, at least. I just have no idea what the rest of it is getting at. Yet. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never explored it in the article, but SCP-2940-B definitely attacks personnel.

the summary you made isn't exactly what I had in mind, but I don't feel the need to correct it since it's a perfectly valid explanation. I did want to correct this, though;

I assume the facility is leaking into our world or something of the sort, bringing -B, or rather, its "shadow", with it.

The bunker was a service that LCE invented, along with some of the other products (SCP-1920 or SCP-1740). People could rent (yup, rent) it for a temporary shelter from their apocalyptic world. The bunker is real and exists in our world after being thrown out of LCE's world.

And in actuality, there's no mention anywhere of -B being invincible. To be honest, he's quiet fragile and could easily be killed. The Foundation simply isn't doing anything because he's part of the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's more of what Hawkin was asking about, earlier on this discussion page, there's not really any time loop per se. I could and will try to revise the explanation in the future, since I handled it too lightly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ directed at Eta Carinae
- Title for hiding text

What is Light Courier Enterprises? What exactly did it do? What is it doing now, and why? Not clear!

I guess I'll either have to cross link this to SCP-1940, SCP-1920, and SCP-1740, or get a tag. Probably the latter.

How did the Foundation discover that skip?

It's irrelevant and otherwise boring.

How is it possible to explore/install surveillance on Floor 10 if SCP-2940-B prevents access to it by attacking any living creatures on the previous floors? Not clear!

By means of the temporal anomaly. Putting a camera in Floor 1 keeps it there, though it does not actively record because of the anomaly. Footage was still obtainable from Floor 8, through usage of the MTF. (I'll elaborate this.)

Why Aokigahara? not clear! And that's too bad, because I was looking forward to reading a skip about the Suicide Forest…

Sorry about that. Maybe I or someone else will write a skip on that place, it was interesting to research.

To be honest, though, the bunker was supposed to manifest randomly on Earth from the Light Courier Enterprises Earth. I chose the place, because it was out of the way and allowed the anomaly to exist and develop several years before the Foundation could even find evidence of it existing there.

Why does every living quarter have a kitchen if food is prepared and provided elsewhere?

Nice catch, meant to say Dining Room.

How long has this been going on?

If you mean the SCP in general, an indefinite amount of time because SCP-2940-B fucked everyone over.

Where does the food come from if SCP-2940 is sealed shut?

10th floor, the apparatus I mentioned in the paragraph about it produces it. It was designed to go to one floor, but the temporal anomaly, again, screws everything up, and 9 versions of everything exists simultaneously.

How does time pass for Foundation observers in their observation of 2940, if it does not flow normally inside SCP-2940?

I saddled to the time description. People still move, but time is immeasurable.

Is the situation still evolving now? Are all 23 original instances of 2940-A dead?

Nothing is evolving. Think of each floor as a snapshot in time. Floor 9 is the last we know of all of them.

Why introduce SCP-2940-B? The situation of SCP-2940 is weird enough not to need any human corpse with a falcon's head on top of it… It only makes everything more confusing, I think.

etc.!!!
You have a point. It'd be totally possible to make this solely about LCE, and a different source for the water impurity. But Ananias' role as the Light Courier would then remain unfulfilled, and that's not what I originally envisioned. I can only offer my apologies if you feel he shouldn't be in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for not getting to this sooner, TL333s.

+ show text
- Title for hiding text

That's not nearly enough for ten stories, unless there's some unmentioned spatial anomaly going on here. The later line "External measurements of SCP-2940 also show that only two floors are present from the outside." explains the height discrepancy, but you might want to mention it sooner.

You have a point, but I'm afraid I'll have to leave it as is. Seems no matter what way I organize this, it's going to be confusing, since the anomalies' (the structure and the occupants) effects are dependent on each other.

Also, what do you mean "from the outside"? You can't really see the floors when you're outside this thing, because underground.

There are methods to do so out there.

This makes it sound like you can leave a watch on floor 1, go to floor 3, and see the passage of time. Things like this may be why people are so confused by this article.

I'll switch around the description of the properties, that might help

You've already introduced the -A's as "families of three to five (each referred to as Group A through E)", which is confusing.

You are finding a lot of important problems.

"The cranium has been replaced with a fleshless skull, identified as Falco rusticolus, and both arms have wings extending from the Humerus. The plumage is also identifiable as that of Falco rusticolus."

Using part of this. I don't want to have to say Falco rusticolus more than twice, and I already mention earlier that it's part of the that bird's skeletal system.

Also, it occurs to me that there's no reason for the Foundation to be treating this as one entity spread across multiple places. Why isn't it described as 10 identical entities, with increasing hostility, etc.? You could almost interpret this as literally just one entity that somehow follows Foundation personnel, teleports between floors or something, and gets more hostile the further down they are because reasons.

That's a neat idea, but it conflicts with the original image and story I had for the article.

The description of how it kills people really isn't all that interesting, and may be a good place to cut a lot of wordiness. We can just sort of assume "big freaky dead bird man kills dudes good", unless those descriptions are really important somehow.

Will edit.

This strikes me oddly. Why not just 'weeping'?

I never clarify if the thing is a man or not, it just uses a male body. Thought I would just leave in there to clarify.

Also, if they can't get to floor 9, how do they get to floor 10?

The stairwell connecting all floors is like that of any other stairwell; stairs leading to each floor, with a doorway (like a hospital or an office building).

apparatuses

I was unsure about this, since the definition of apparatus implies it refers to all technical equipment of one mechanism, which would be the bunker. I guess I'll change it.

all that other stuff

Yup, changed

Honestly, I don't see how this pertains to Pitch Haven at all, and if any of those details link to it, I can't find the connection.

This is because I decided to not use any of Fantem's characters, since I didn't want to mess with the plot he was developing. I am writing a tale to connect this article and 1530, as Ananias, Salix, and Willow were all Edenites. In fact, I didn't know Fantem was writing a canon using all his articles until he told me.

I appreciate the in depth review, you should do them more. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to know. The wave-function that is my comprehension can now collapse into a coherent particle. A series of Increasingly-Less-Pleasantvilles created from chunks of time during The Event.

item.title: Small fix
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2940-B dispatches each subject by seizing them and bringing them near it's skull

It's should be its. Small grammatical error, but I like this skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For some reason, I really wanted to give this one some detailed critique.

+ Thoughts:
- Thoughts:

8 meters

That's not nearly enough for ten stories, unless there's some unmentioned spatial anomaly going on here. The later line "External measurements of SCP-2940 also show that only two floors are present from the outside." explains the height discrepancy, but you might want to mention it sooner. Also, what do you mean "from the outside"? You can't really see the floors when you're outside this thing, because underground.

a watch left within Floor 1 can be found in Floor 3, measuring the passage of approximately 92 days.

This makes it sound like you can leave a watch on floor 1, go to floor 3, and see the passage of time. Things like this may be why people are so confused by this article.

Time-measuring devices are inoperative

Which kind of undoes your example above.

SCP-2940-A refers to all human individuals and families residing within SCP-2940, which includes twenty-three persons.

You've already introduced the -A's as "families of three to five (each referred to as Group A through E)", which is confusing.

SCP-2940-B's skull is capable of luminescence and incandescence, presumably through a non-biological process.

The part after the comma really isn't necessary.

The majority of SCP-2940-B is composed of a male human corpse.

You've already told us this with "SCP-2940-B is a human cadaver". Some combining would be in order. It might also be a good idea to describe the lacerations and so forth before the avian parts (suggestion: "The cranium has been replaced with a fleshless skull, identified as Falco rusticolus, and both arms have wings extending from the Humerus. The plumage is also identifiable as that of Falco rusticolus.") because that makes a natural progression from least strange to most strange. Also, it would remove the physical description from the paragraph describing behavior.

SCP-2940-B displays increasing hostility towards living organisms following each floor of SCP-2940

I think a better way to phrase this would be "SCP-2940-B displays increasing hostility towards living organisms, correlated to its depth within SCP-2940". The "following each floor" phrase doesn't really make any sense, and might be another thing confusing people.

Also, it occurs to me that there's no reason for the Foundation to be treating this as one entity spread across multiple places. Why isn't it described as 10 identical entities, with increasing hostility, etc.? You could almost interpret this as literally just one entity that somehow follows Foundation personnel, teleports between floors or something, and gets more hostile the further down they are because reasons.

The description of how it kills people really isn't all that interesting, and may be a good place to cut a lot of wordiness. We can just sort of assume "big freaky dead bird man kills dudes good", unless those descriptions are really important somehow.

degenerate

This is not the right word. How about "75% (or whatever percentage you want to use, even black boxes; actually, why isn't it all of them?) of subjects' remains will convert to 12 liters of the substance exuded by SCP-2940-B."

Again, unless the description is important, how it consumes the stuff can be cut.

Masculine weeping

This strikes me oddly. Why not just 'weeping'?

Also, if they can't get to floor 9, how do they get to floor 10?

Brother is now missing from Group E, Living room is in noticeable disarray.

Either change the comma to a period, or uncapitalize "Living".

Floor 10 contains several apparatus

apparatuses

I think the water finally got to them, but at least they're all breathing.

Doesn't make sense, where's the logical connection between these phrases?

Dangit, now you've got -B going between floors, it sounds like it can always do this.

Small quantities of smoke are emitted from the doorway,

This isn't phrased quite right. Is this emitting from Floor 7's doorway? If so, you should take the last sentence of the previous paragraph and put it before this, so the connection is more clear.

it is unknown if the regenerative properties of said substance incorrectly regenerates all affected instances.

Take out "regenerative", it's redundant.

As for stuff to cut, I gave some suggestions. Honestly, I don't see how this pertains to Pitch Haven at all, and if any of those details link to it, I can't find the connection. That said, you could always justify their inclusion by making them more obviously relevant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If that's the case, the article could at least put "time loop" in there somewhere. Otherwise it's (IMO) being needlessly obtuse — there's no single, intuitive way for something to "have experienced the passage of 46 days in time", and the example given doesn't imply a time loop at all.

Maybe I'm just being dense, but temporal fuckery SCPs should be handled much more carefully than this one was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not quite getting all the complaints. Sure, the description is muddy in a few places, but the overall effect is pretty clear.

+If this is incorrect feel free to correct me Accelerando
– hide block

It's an underground shelter. Each floor is like a snapshot in time and the events inside loop indefinitely. Each floor is like the previous floor, except 46 days in the future, so Floor 2 depicts the events that occurred 46 days after the events on Floor 1, Floor 3 depicts the events that occurred 92 days after Floor 1, etc.- with the exception of Floor 10, which is a storage room of sorts. When all the notable events have occurred in the period of time that that particular floor is depicting, the events reset and loop. The inhabitants of each floor can interact with outside people but it doesn't affect key events from happening on the floor, and upon reset they won't remember anything. -B is some sort of apocalyptic entity that was wreaking havoc on the world that this facility came from, and Light Courier Enterprises attempted to build the shelter to hide people from it, which obviously didn't work. The reason -B doesn't attack Foundation researchers is because it's stuck in the time loop. I assume the facility is leaking into our world or something of the sort, bringing -B, or rather, its "shadow", with it.


Throroughly enjoyed this skip and its atmosphere, even if I had to reread once or twice. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am hopelessly confused by this SCP. It was difficult to read it through - I kept hoping for a sign or signs that the confusion was deliberate (the situation described here is indeed very complex and confusing)… but I didn't see any sign - which of course, does not mean they're not there!
I agree with Petrograd's comments, with the personal precision that there are much more 'clarity' issues than quoted, IMO.

+ show block
– hide block

What is Light Courier Enterprises? What exactly did it do? What is it doing now, and why? Not clear!
How did the Foundation discover that skip? How is it possible to explore/install surveillance on Floor 10 if SCP-2940-B prevents access to it by attacking any living creatures on the previous floors? Not clear!
Why Aokigahara? not clear! And that's too bad, because I was looking forward to reading a skip about the Suicide Forest…
Why does every living quarter have a kitchen if food is prepared and provided elsewhere?
How long has this been going on? Where does the food come from if SCP-2940 is sealed shut? How does time pass for Foundation observers in their observation of 2940, if it does not flow normally inside SCP-2940? Is the situation still evolving now? Are all 23 original instances of 2940-A dead?
Why introduce SCP-2940-B? The situation of SCP-2940 is weird enough not to need any human corpse with a falcon's head on top of it… It only makes everything more confusing, I think.
etc.!!!

Downvote. I feel bad about it because it could only be me not 'getting' this skip, but downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I strongly dislike this and explained why on reddit.

Falco Lombardi should not be in the article, and the temporal anomaly is poorly established. The lack of human factor severely detracts from horror. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got the gist of it, but it feels like it's really weirdly written. I couldn't understand what was happening with the time skip floor deal until I read the comments, which isn't a good thing. I like what I read enough so I won't downvote, but I won't upvote either because of what I mentioned before, and because that last use of [REDACTED] (the first one was at least partially explained, but that last one seemed lazy).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The length of a text has no relation to its quality. I personally think this is as long as it needs to be- there's a lot of really nice, convoluted stuff going on here, so its going to take quite a bit of explaining to cover it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, so, I downvoted because I was already confused by the description of the temporal anomaly when the real meat of the addenda hit. Upon a reread I like it better (I really enjoyed the event log and the third image did creep me out. I don't really enjoy that, but that's the goal, so urah!), but I'm keeping the -1 for clarity issues. Here are some areas I noticed.

+ show block
– hide block

These are in order of their appearance in the article. I'll mark them Grammar and Clarity as I go. The Clarity issues are far more important.

SCP-2940 has ten floors, each floor containing five residences consisting in families of three to five (each referred to as Group A through E).

Grammar, "in" should probably be "of".

Uninhabited rooms in SCP-2940 exhibit considerable disrepair though all rooms inhabited by instances of SCP-2940-A are generally clean and fit for living.

Grammar, I think it needs a comma before though.

However, testing during containment has shown that time does not actively pass within any floor of SCP-2940 (excluding Floor 10).

Clarity, and a major issue. I don't understand this even one bit. I understand each floor consisting of the previous floor plus 46 days, but you also have records of personnel interacting with -A instances and recording surveillance footage of events on several floors as they occur. These are things that absolutely require the subjective passage of time.

If you mean that time stands still in some more metaphysical or abstract technical sense, this sentence does not suffice to convey that. If you meant something more specific (e.g. they always have the same start point when you examine them?), you need to state that more directly, as at the moment I can't even begin to guess what you meant.

(See Addendum 2940-LB4).

Clarity. You don't have an addendum -LB4, if this was meant to point to an existing one. Otherwise, I don't mind people referencing unprovided addenda, but to me it is an issue here because 1) You have a lot of addenda with similar titles, so people scrolling down to look for it might be confused, and 2) the temporal anomaly was already confusing enough that I was really looking forward to some kind of clarification. If you fixed the sentence indicated in the last greybox, this probably isn't as big of a deal.

No attempts to prevent the outcome of all instances of SCP-2940-A have been made, due to the possibility of unforeseen consequences.

Clarity. You make it clear further in that all of the -As have died in horrible fashions as the floors get lower, but just "outcome" is vague and suggests that the -A instances are some kind of anomaly/process in and of themselves. Them all dying seems to be the outcome of SCP-2940 in general.

Instances of SCP-2940-A are unable to exit SCP-2940 and develop physical abnormalities following death, mainly in lower floors of SCP-2940.

Clarity, and a relatively minor point. Does more abnormalities appear in lower floors, or is it just that in lower floors everyone is dead?

SCP-2940-B is a human cadaver that gains autonomy throughout each floor within SCP-2940.

Clarity. I think it would be better to specify that -B gains increasing autonomy with floor depth/"time". I think that that's your intention, but not directly stated in this wording.

SCP-2940-B displays increasing hostility towards life and attacks all organisms within eyesight, lacking all other sensory systems.

Clarity. Does -B display more hostility over floor depth/"time"? Does -B display hostility towards SCP Task Force personnel, and if so how was it observed in the logs?

All subjects are dispatched when seized by and brought near SCP-2940-B, which increases the brightness and heat of its skull to temperatures above 750 Celsius, causing third-degree burns to develop on the subject's head and upper body.

Grammar. The wording here is just a bit strange. Dispatched is a little detached even for clinical language, and including "seized by" and "brought near" seem redundant. I mean, you can't really do one without the other. If you wanted to specify that -B seizes them and brings them near it's skull, active voice would probably be more effective. E.g. "SCP-2940-B dispatches each subject by seizing them and bringing them near it's skull, which increases in brightness and heat…"

Lower floors MTF Rho-2 directly observed SCP-2940-B behavior.

Grammar. Improper sentence structure.

as the entrance of your neighborhood of five will be fully strengthened will be completely nonexistent.

Grammar, as you've got two "will be" phrases there. If the grammar in this portion is supposed to be weird, please ignore this! I enjoy weird grammar from weird otherworldly GOIs.

Grief is permitted upon complement of the transaction!

Grammar (word choice). Complement? Again, ignore this if it's supposed to be weird!

Edit: Thank you for the edits, I think I have a clearer picture now. Changed to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely the second, except it's not so pleasant in the floors where there's perpetual chaos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, so I thought of three comparisons to try to figure out what is going on here, as far as what an observer would see happening in the various floors. Having read the modified article, comparison one is obsolete, but I'll include it nonetheless to illustrate my thought process.

Comparison One: Groundhog Day - A single day/time period endlessly loops with one or more people able to influence events through their actions during said time period, only to see them reset at the end of the loop period. But most people are unaware of the loop, and so act out the exact same actions repeatedly unless interfered with.
Similar examples: Edge of Tomorrow, Run Lola Run, a billion others (obsolete as of revision)

Comparison 2: Pleasantville - "Time" passes in normal cycles (i.e. each day is different from the next, weekends follow work weeks, etc.) at the same speed for all observers but nothing ever really changes. No one ages, relocates, or changes occupation, and social, economic, political, and technological standards never change.
Similar examples: Dante's Inferno, What Dreams May Come, Silent Hill (movie)

Comparison 3: The Dark Tower - "Time" is meaningless, and can stretch, compress, loop, and have gaps, often varying by individual. One party may act as if a previous meeting never happened (the "forgetting" party reset to an earlier time, or the other looped back to an earlier version of the "forgetting" party). Time may stretch for one person and/or compress for another, so that the second appears to age rapidly to the first, and the first appears to stop aging to the second.
Similar examples: Limbo in Inception, others I can't think of right now

Anyhow, all that is to say: Which of the two remaining comparisons fits better (if either)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Streamlined parts of the article and the temporal effects. Considering the article is half of the size of 1619's, I don't really know what other details to cut, since they all pertain to the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm writing a tale to connect this and SCP-1530, which is where Salix and Willow are. Can't guarantee it'll be anytime soon, but it's coming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this definitely feels like pitch haven, but I can't find any clear links between this and the rest of the stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really long, and worse, really wordy. I think you're trying to explain too much of what's going on, with Foundation scientists "surmising" the explanation. There's a lot you could cut out and still have it be interesting, plus this needs some editing for flow. I'll also note that the -B wasn't terribly interesting as an entity until we got to the description of its behavior. Neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most like "always Tuesday", for floors 1-9.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really good and creepy. That third picture terrified me, mutated ghost faces are what absolutely freak me the hell out. Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to like this, but I have no goddamn clue how to interpret any of the temporal shit or how it relates to the rest of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Freaking love this.

There is still one thing I'm trying to figure out (I think this might be part of what Hawkin was trying to ask too). Is time stopped like a snapshot on each level until there's an observer? Or are they in some sort of "its always tuesday" stasis where you can interact with them and they go through their routines but its basically an unaging loop? Or is the situation still running in real time (just staggered), in which case the total chaos will eventually work its way up to floor 1?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You got the Pitch Haven feel down stat, and even on its own, it's a goddamn fantastic SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking great. Goddamn. I haven't felt this creeped over a new SCP in a long while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll clarify this further in the article, but each floor is a projection of a certain period of time within SCP-2940. The logs of the employee was when the actual events on each floor were occurring, though -B was only faintly manifested within the bunker. The Foundation is containing a fragmented remainder of the bunker's reality that leaked into our world, and now -B is a fully realized entity that is forcing all the occupants to experience the last years of their lives indefinitely.

Kind of a lot to swallow, so I'll edit accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, okay, now it all makes sense. That part wasn't very clear. Perhaps modifying this passage…

Beginning at Floor 1, each subsequent Floor appears to experience the passage of 46 days in time; for example, a watch left within Floor 1 can be found in Floor 3, measuring the passage of approximately 92 days; Floor 9 is presumably a future projection of Floor 1, 414 days in time.

…to better indicate that each floor is a set period ahead of the previous? I know I'm kinda new and don't want to step on any toes, but from my own work (away from the site) it's usually true that if one person doesn't understand, several others don't either.

Knowing that part really helps with getting the hook and the creep in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am also a bit confused by the lower-floors-are-future-copies bit. If floor 9 was an inaccessible hellhole upon discovery, does that mean floor 1 would be that inaccessible hellhole 414 days later?

In regard to the watch example, what happens if you take the watch from floor 3 out, then remove the watch from floor 1? Also in that vein, did they only set up surveillance once and thus get video/audio from all floors simultaneously from one set of equipment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Moved a paragraph (talks about onlybuilding first, then -A, then -B) and touched up wording a bit. Awkward writing has always been a problem for me, the way I read it probably makes it seems normal only to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Each floor is the previous floor in the future, so they have the same structure and such ('cept the 10th one). And yeah, dead people tend to act inconsistently weird in SCP-2940, not much other ways to describe it.

Glad you liked it. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good, but not that well organized IMO. The description should put all the info on each topic (the building, the people, etc.) in the same section and not keep jumping around. Also, some of the word choice is awkward (bear inability to exit - just try 'are unable to exit' or 'lack the ability to exit') just for one example.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
develop physical abnormalities and autonomy while deceased in lower floors

Deceased? Is that a typo or do they die and come back? Also the specimens develop autonomy? Just seems unclear.

I'm a little confused about the floors. Is each floor identical to the one above, including occupants and then things get weirder the further down you go? That's my reading.

Overall, I like it. It's creepy, weird and confusing in a good way.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hideaway, hideaway
Where they'll never say you cannot stay
Come and play, my hideaway
Someday everything will be okay

This article involves Pitch Haven and my Light Courier Enterprises (you can start reading it from SCP-1940 or checking out the tale). They aren't necessarily directly involved with each other; the bunker's occupants are the victims here. Ananias's presence is what's splitting the floors apart and degrading the occupants of the bunker.

First image is from here, second one is a conglomeration of several images, but the third one… I can't find a credible source.

BIG thanks to Faminepulse for creating the second image and tweaking the third image, he is an image wizard. Many thanks also go to Professor Will, TwistedGears, Procyonlotor, and nulljellyfish for reviewing, and of course Fantem for making Pitch Haven. :)
